android.title: Fortnite
android.summary: Battle royale, PvP, social games and more. Compete solo or play with friends.
android.description:
There is always something new for you and your friends in Fortnite!

Be the last player standing in Battle Royale. Get nostalgic in Fortnite OG. Headline a concert in Fortnite Festival. Explore the worlds of LEGO Fortnite. Play thousands of free developer-made multiplayer adventure games like tycoons, deathruns, zombie survival, and more. There’s always something new for you and your friends.

In between action-packed games, stop by the Shop to get an Outfit, Back Bling, or Emote. Show off your new look as you go no-building, all action in Zero Build, or get tactical in Reload. Brawl and play Ranked modes and qualify for Fortnite Competitive tournaments, where you can earn rewards like cash or cosmetics.

There’s a new Battle Pass every Season, with tons of rewards you can use across Fortnite, like new Outfits, Pickaxes, and Gliders. Unlock these rewards as you complete Quests and earn XP.

Past and present collide in the OG Pass, where you can unlock classic looks with fresh Outfits inspired by past Seasons as you brawl your opponents.

New Seasons of Fortnite Festival bring a brand new music Icon to the stage. Perform as your favorite artists with their Outfits and hit songs in the Shop and Music Pass.

WHAT’S NEW:
- Overwatch is here! Your mission: protect the island. Answer the call with Overwatch heroes D.Va, Genji, Tracer, and Mercy now in Fortnite.
- Zero Build is better than ever with new ways to move, fight, and survive.

GAMES IN THE FORTNITE MOBILE APP:
- Battle Royale
- Blitz Royale
- Zero Build
- Fortnite OG
- LEGO Fortnite
- Fortnite Festival
- Adventure Games
- Developer-Made Games
- Competitive Games
- & more

ACTION PACKED BATTLE ROYALE, WHEREVER & WHENEVER
- Choose your Outfit and explore the Island in style
- Fight to be the last one standing in this fast-paced third-person PvP shooter
- Avoid damage as the Storm closes in
- Coming soon: updates bringing new weapons, vehicles, and more to our competitive games

BRAWL & COMPETE SOLO OR TEAM UP IN ZERO BUILD
- Battle Royale without the building. Play solo or team up as a duo, trio, or 4-person squad
- Use your Overshield for protection because no building means more danger
- Use natural coverage as you move through the map, and leverage your loadout to the max
- Mantle, hurdle, jump, and more to stay competitive

MORE TO EXPLORE
- Play faster in a smaller map with fewer players in Blitz Royale
- Jump into social adventure games
- Perform or remix awesome music with Jam Tracks from the Shop or Music Pass

It’s all right here, in your pocket.
android.recentChanges: Jump into Shattered, the End of Season Live Event, at 7pm ET on June 5th.

ios.title: Fortnite
ios.description:
Play and battle with friends in Fortnite. Be the last player standing in Battle Royale, Zero Build, Blitz and OG, carry your squad to victory in fast-paced Reload, build your own adventures in LEGO® Fortnite, or headline a concert in Fortnite Festival — it's all here.

Play thousands of free developer-made islands with your friends, including tycoons, deathruns, zombie survival, and more.

DISCLAIMER:
Fortnite can be played on iPhone 11 and newer, iPhone XS and newer, iPhone 12 Mini and newer, iPhone SE 3 and newer, iPad Pro 10.5" and newer, iPad Air 4th Gen and newer, iPad 10th Gen and newer, iPad Mini 6th Gen and newer.

as far as I have seen it has ran great for a phone. I logged in smoothly with no troubles. I also love how I you can just download the modes you want. some games take up more space than others so beware some are over 5 GB. But overall it has improved a lot since I played back in 2017 on mobile. CAUTION ⚠️: some models of phones will heat up fast.

Fortnite is a great game. I've played it on several different consoles such as a Nintendo, PlayStation, PC, etc. Since they released the mobile version, I had to give it a try. To begin with, the graphics aren't that great, but you can change them in settings, though you sacrifice FPS, which I recommend for Lego Fortnite Oddyssey. Additionally, if you play the Blitz and Battle Royale modes, it feels great as a mobile game. If played on new gen phones, then this is a great shooter. Good game.

So far it's a smooth five all day on mobile for me! going to be playing daily, used to play only on PC a few years ago and have been missing this game. hoping you guys keep up the fun battle passes and keep this game going for a long time to come! will definitely be a paying customer. -New_Nu

Best Battle Royal game at least for Smartphones. It has a lot Maps,Modes,Items and most Collab of any game in existence. There is so much to play so much to explore that if you know you will never get bored and it will almost never get Repetitive. Well there are some drawbacks like it requires a good device for better performance and in many areas there is no server so high ping issues but overall for Smartphone it's a 5/5 no cap.

literally my favorite video videogame of all time, it's a little hard to play on mobile but you can pair a controller via Bluetooth and it plays as well as it does on PS4 or Nintendo Switch so not too bad. I have a Samsung Z Fold phone and it is really cool to be able to play on a larger screen with a controller anywhere!

Fortnite is one of the best battle royale games ever made. The game has amazing graphics, smooth gameplay, and nonstop action that keeps it exciting every time you play. What makes Fortnite special is its unique building system, which makes fights more creative and competitive compared to other shooting games. The game also gets regular updates with new skins, weapons, events, and collaborations with famous movies, artists, and superheroes. The maps are always changing, so it never feels boring.

fortnite is a bloated game whether you play on console or PC. so the fact that they were able to create a mobile version that is exactly the same as the two other platforms is remarkable. you're able to play on an Android or Apple in the same match as someone who's playing on PC. the only challenge is the optimization, but the developer has included plenty of settings that you can toy with to get it to work on your device. not many other games have accomplished this.

I downloaded the game, went through set up and logged into my Epic account and then it force reset my phone. now it won't show up on my phone but shows it's still installed in the app store but won't let me uninstall it. *Update, re-downloaded after the new season and everything is working great. It runs just like console if you have a controller for your phone and isn't bad on touchpad either.

Fortnite is an amazing game overall. The graphics are impressive, colorful, and smooth, making the gameplay experience really enjoyable. Everything from the character designs to the maps feels well-made and engaging. The game runs well and offers a lot of fun, whether you're playing solo or with friends. However, one major issue I’ve noticed is the downloading speed, especially when it comes to map updates. Even with a fast internet connection, the map downloads can be surprisingly slow, which c

Love this game. I've put maybe over 200 hours on it. I wish they would give us more customization options just like PC, especially on the graphics side of the game. I still think this is the best game on my phone.

I’m blown away by how well Fortnite runs on mobile. It isn’t a stripped-down version; it’s the full, high-quality experience I play on my console, synced perfectly via cross-progression. Whether I’m using touch controls or a Bluetooth controller, the performance is smooth, the graphics look sharp, and I never miss out on live events or season updates. It’s the ultimate way to play on the go!

fortnight is one of the most popular video games in the world today. Created by Epic Games and released in 2017, the game quickly became a global sensation among teenagers and adults. Fortnite is mainly known for its Battle Royale mode, where one hundred players compete against each other until only one player or team remains. However, Fortnite is more than just a game about winning. It has changed entertainment, social interaction, and even education in many ways.

This game is an absolute masterpiece in the world of online gaming. From its vibrant graphics to its fast-paced gameplay, it delivers an experience that’s both exciting and endlessly entertaining. One of the best things about Fortnite is its constant updates. New seasons, skins, events, and collaborations keep the game feeling alive and relevant

The only things that puts me off of the game at times are the decisions made by epic games, the company who developed fortnite. A prime example of a bad change being the update to the price of the battle pass, vbucks and what you actually get from crew. Youre paying the same as before for less. Anyway, still have fun. Just take it from me, Ive been playing for nearly 9 years.

if you were wondering why the pistol in fortnite got turned into an automatic SMG and there are no more semi-autopistols in fortnite, it's because of this mobile game adaptation. apparently the mobile game players that are playing this game are also playing cross play with Xbox PlayStation Nintendo and PC. because of this reason it's hard to aim and open fire while being on a mobile screen so they turn the pistol into an automatic pistol to fix that. it's cool.

I love Fortnite and I play on an iPad M4, but the lag and FPS drops make the game really hard to enjoy sometimes. Fortnite Mobile needs better performance settings or a real “Performance Mode” for mobile players. I don’t understand why this is happening on such a powerful device because older Fortnite Mobile used to run way smoother than this.

The biggest issue is during fights, especially in Zone Wars or fast endgames. Every time I fight someone, the FPS drops and the game starts lagging, which usually gets me eliminated instantly. It’s already difficult playing against PC and console players, and the lag just makes it worse. If the performance was stable, the game would actually feel fun and competitive again.

I’ve already tried everything to fix it on my side, so I really hope Epic improves optimization for mobile devices. I genuinely love this game and just want Fortnite Mobile to feel smooth again like it used to.

A lot needs to change. Gamemodes need to be more fleshed out and worked on. Whatever doesn't work needs to be removed. More accessibility, like an FOV Slider and graphic settings for console for performance, is needed. The engine needs more fine-tuning. The art style needs to be more like the early chapters. Cosmetic rarities need to come back. The UI needs to look more like the old UI. FOMO needs to be gone, and old cosmetics need to be made available, with exclusive styles given to og owners

After spending a considerable amount of time downloading a hefty 10GB file, I found myself in a situation where I had nothing to play, as each individual game required a separate download process. This scenario could be significantly improved by implementing a more streamlined solution, such as the addition of an 'Add Download All' button. Such a feature would enable users to effortlessly queue all their desired game files for download at once, allowing us the freedom to manage our time better a

I decided to try this game and downloaded it with interest. However, during the account creation process, I noticed something very concerning. The game asked for not only my email ID but also the actual password of my email account to create a login. This is a serious security risk. No legitimate game or platform should ever ask for your personal email password. Because of this, I did not feel safe continuing with the registration process. I’ve been a loyal Fortnite player for the last two years and have invested over $2,000 into this game because I genuinely enjoy the experience. However, after this latest update, the game has become almost unplayable for me.
The number of bugs in this current build is frustrating, but the most game-breaking issue is the weapon swapping bug. Multiple times mid-fight, I’ve found myself unable to switch weapons, which essentially ruins any chance of competing in a match. Between these technical failures and the general performance instability since the update, I can no longer justify the time or money I’ve put into this game.
I really want to love this game again, but in its current state, it feels like the quality control has completely vanished. Please address these stability issues and the weapon swap bug. Until the game is fixed, I’ll be taking a break.

It's overall a great game and fun game that feels fresh and new with every chapter. For my mid-range device (Galaxy A50), it runs horribly, with very inconsistent fps with lots of stutter and hitching. Needs better optimization for older devices. For instance, I am using FXAA and 30% resolution and can barely hit 20 fps. Will rate higher when optimization is better.

Mostly playable, but it's a glitchy mess. Often get an "unknown error" trying to join matches and have to relaunch the game to fix it. It also crashes at the results screen at the end of every match. Once you're in a match it runs fine, but getting into and out of a match requires working around things breaking. Also, it looks like garbage no matter how powerful your phone is. Lazy devs clearly took the easy way out and only support potato phones instead of optimizing for different phones.

Fortnite is a game that I have played for a few years, and when I finally got Fortnite mobile, I was slightly disappointed. First of all, it took FOREVER to download. Second of all, games take forever to download in Fortnite. Even with these problems, I still love Fortnite. I also recommend playing on a newer phone because from my experience with IPhone 13, it is almost unplayable. The only reason this rating isn’t lower is because it is probably meant for newer devices and not older ones. I hope this helps people decide if they want to download Fortnite or not.
